description: Lock the direct prerequisite boundary for the operational January 1983 TCP/IP Internet while preserving its broader networking ancestry transitively.

checks.reason: Personal computers were optional endpoint products, not a universal condition of the 1983 host transition.
Generic telecommunications remains transitive background; packet switching is the directly specified network mechanism.
The operational ARPA Internet joined satellite, packet-radio, and other networks without requiring fiber.
TCP/IP transports arbitrary application octets without a required compression stage.
Computer networking is already preserved transitively through both mechanism-specific direct prerequisites.
RFC 791 defines the internetwork over interconnected packet-switched computer communication networks.
Packet-switched networks and gateways are constitutive of the scoped 1983 architecture.
RFC 801 defines completed IP/TCP deployment as the condition for Full Internet Service.
The node's definition and January 1983 milestone are specifically TCP/IP-based.
The broad networking ancestry must remain available transitively without a redundant direct edge.
RFC 801 labels January 1983 as Full Internet Service after the complete NCP-to-IP/TCP transition.
